questions.question: How many brothers did the young seagull have?
questions.explanation: The text states: 'His two brothers and his sister had already flown away.'

questions.question: The young seagull's sister's wings were _______ compared to his.
questions.explanation: 'His little sister, whose wings were far shorter than his own.'

questions.question: How long had the seagull been without food?
questions.explanation: 'He felt the heat because he had not eaten since the previous nightfall.'

questions.question: What dried food did the seagull find on the ledge?
questions.explanation: 'He had found a dried piece of mackerel tail at the far end of his ledge.'

questions.question: What direction did the seagull's ledge face?
questions.explanation: 'His ledge that faced south.'

questions.question: What was the father seagull doing when the young seagull tried to get attention?
questions.explanation: 'His father was preening the feathers on his white back.'

questions.question: What colour was the father seagull's back?
questions.explanation: 'His father was preening the feathers on his white back.'

questions.question: What colour was the mother seagull's breast?
questions.explanation: 'Standing on a little high hump on the plateau, her white breast thrust forward.'

questions.question: What colour were the young seagull's legs?
questions.explanation: 'His long grey legs stepping daintily.'

questions.question: The young seagull's first fish seen being caught was a _______.
questions.explanation: 'He had seen his older brother catch his first herring and devour it.'

questions.question: Where was the big plateau where the family gathered?
options: at the top of the cliff
questions.explanation: 'The big plateau midway down the opposite cliff.'

questions.question: What scraps of food did the family offer after the seagull's first flight?
questions.explanation: 'Their beaks were offering him scraps of dog-fish.'

questions.question: When was Liam O'Flaherty born?
questions.explanation: The textbook states: 'Liam O'Flaherty (1896–1984).'

questions.question: When did Liam O'Flaherty die?
questions.explanation: The textbook states: 'Liam O'Flaherty (1896–1984).'

questions.question: Liam O'Flaherty was from _______.
questions.explanation: 'Liam O'Flaherty was an Irish novelist and short story writer.'

questions.question: How many legs was the seagull standing on when trying to get attention?
questions.explanation: 'Standing on one leg with the other leg hidden under his wing.'

questions.question: The mother screamed '______' mockingly at the young seagull.
questions.explanation: 'Gawl-ool-ah,' she screamed back mockingly.

questions.question: What colour was the sea described as when the seagull flew over it?
questions.explanation: 'He saw a vast green sea beneath him.' and 'He dropped his legs to stand on the green sea.'

questions.question: The seagull gnawed at _______ when desperately hungry.
questions.explanation: 'He even gnawed at the dried pieces of eggshell. It was like eating part of himself.'

questions.question: Two constant themes in O'Flaherty's works are _______.
questions.explanation: 'Two constant themes in his works are nature and Ireland.'
